title: Prompt Compression
learning_outcomes: Explain token pruning thresholds based on information content.

content_sections.title: Prompt Redundancy and Perplexity
content_sections.content: Natural language prompts often contain redundant words. To reduce latency and save token costs, prompt compression algorithms (such as LLMLingua) compute the perplexity or entropy of document segments using a small language model. Segments with low perplexity (which are highly predictable and convey less unique information) are pruned, while high-perplexity segments are preserved.
